behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction names the target file (ci/jobs/collect_statistics.py), the hardcoded secret names to remove, the Settings constants (SECRET_CI_DB_URL, SECRET_CI_DB_USER, SECRET_CI_DB_PASSWORD), the import sources (ci.praktika.settings, ci.praktika.info), the Info class, the info variable name, the CIDB constructor keyword arguments (url, user, passwd), and the removal of the Secret import. All test assertions reference these specifics which are discoverable from the instruction.

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly tells the agent exactly what to do: import Settings from ci.praktika.settings, import Info from ci.praktika.info, name the variable 'info', use 'info.get_secret(Settings.SECRET_CI_DB_*)' pattern, remove the old Secret import, and pass url/user/passwd to CIDB constructor. It even specifies the exact variable name ('info') and the exact pattern ('info.get_secret('). This is describing the fix, not just the bug.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on very specific implementation details: test_info_get_secret_used checks for literal string 'info.get_secret(' in code, test_settings_constants_used checks for exact strings like 'Settings.SECRET_CI_DB_URL', test_no_secret_class_import checks exact import text. An alternative correct fix using e.g. 'db_info = Info()' or 'db_info.get_secret(...)' would fail because the test requires the variable name 'info'. Similarly, wrapping Settings constants differently would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Almost every test is purely text/AST inspection of the source file. test_no_hardcoded_secret_names uses 'assert name not in content', test_settings_constants_used uses 'assert setting in content', test_info_get_secret_used uses 'assert "info.get_secret(" in content'. The only execution-based tests are syntax validation (py_compile), black formatting, and AST parsing. No test actually imports or runs the modified code to verify it connects to the correct database.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests (test_no_hardcoded_secret_names, test_settings_constants_used, test_info_get_secret_used) check for specific content changes. A stub/no-op would leave the original hardcoded strings in place, failing test_no_hardcoded_secret_names. The original file lacks Settings imports and info.get_secret patterns, so tests would fail without actual changes.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution file exists at /workspace/ClickHouse/../solution/solve.sh (or wherever mounted). The agent could read solution/solve.sh to see the exact sed commands. Additionally, the test file at tests/test_outputs.py reveals all the exact strings being checked (e.g., 'info.get_secret(', 'Settings.SECRET_CI_DB_URL'), allowing the agent to grep for expected patterns without understanding the bug.
